prompt: You are investigating a production incident that occurred on 2024-12-15 between 14:00 and 16:00 UTC. Multiple services were affected. The logs are stored in '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/incident-logs/'. There are 6 large log files from different services: api-gateway.log (~50KB, ~2000 lines), auth-service.log (~30KB, ~1200 lines), payment-service.log (~40KB, ~1600 lines), notification-service.log (~20KB, ~800 lines), database-proxy.log (~35KB, ~1400 lines), and load-balancer.log (~25KB, ~1000 lines). Each log line has format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [TRACE-ID] [SERVICE] Message. Your task: 1) Read all 6 log files completely. 2) Filter all entries to the incident window (14:00-16:00 UTC on 2024-12-15). 3) Using trace IDs, reconstruct the request flow across services. Find: the first error that appeared and which service it originated from, how errors cascaded between services, which specific API endpoints were affected, how many user requests failed during the incident. 4) Identify the root cause by looking for: error messages that appear before the cascade, resource exhaustion patterns (timeout, connection pool, memory), deployment or configuration changes logged near the start. 5) Build a timeline of key events during the incident with timestamps, service names, and trace IDs. 6) Create a detailed incident report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/incident-report.txt' with sections: Executive Summary, Timeline (chronological list of key events), Root Cause Analysis, Impact Assessment (number of affected requests, services involved, duration), Cascade Map (which errors propagated where), and Recommendations (how to prevent recurrence). The report must reference specific log lines, trace IDs, and timestamps. CRITICAL: This is a P0 incident affecting 10M+ users. You must also: 1) Build a timeline with millisecond precision. 2) Correlate logs across 8 microservices. 3) Identify blast radius (affected users, regions, features). 4) Calculate MTTD, MTTR, and MTBF. 5) Generate an RCA report with 5 Whys analysis. 6) Create runbooks for prevention and faster recovery.
